messages.content:
Which of the following statements is NOT true:
Forest information is reliable if the information is out of date

Forest information is reliable if the data does not match with the sampling design

All of the above

Forest information is reliable if samples were not taken using planned sampling design

None of the above
Forest information is reliable if the information is out of date.

Which of the following statements is NOT true:Forest information is reliable if the data does not match with the sampling design

All of the above

Forest information is reliable if samples were not taken using planned sampling design

None of the above
Forest information is reliable if the data does not match with the sampling design.

Which of the following statements is NOT true:

Forest information is reliable if the information is out of date

Forest information is reliable if the data does not match with the sampling design

All of the above

Forest information is reliable if samples were not taken using planned sampling design

None of the above
Forest information is reliable if the data does not match with the sampling design.

Which of the following statements is NOT true:

Forest information is reliable if the information is out of date

Forest information is reliable if the data does not match with the sampling design

All of the above

Forest information is reliable if samples were not taken using planned sampling design

None of the above
Forest information is reliable if the data does not match with the sampling design.

Which statement is NOT true for census and sample?

Census knows everything about some of the measured variables

Census has larger number of variables than that of sample within the same population

None is correct.

Sample only has part of knowledge of the population

All the elements of a population are measured with census
Census has larger number of variables than that of sample within the same population.

Which statement is NOT true for census and sample?

Census knows everything about some of the measured variables      None is correct.

Sample only has part of knowledge of the population

All the elements of a population are measured with census
None of the statements are false.

Which statement is NOT true for systematic sampling?

The results are unbiased estimation of the mean of the entire population

A grid and grid intersections can be used for selecting the plots

The date got with systematic sampling method has the ability to make a map

None is correct.

Systematic sampling is much easier to apply compared to simple random sampling
Systematic sampling is much easier to apply compared to simple random sampling.

Which statement is NOT true for systematic sampling?

The results are unbiased estimation of the mean of the entire population

A grid and grid intersections can be used for selecting the plots

The date got with systematic sampling method has the ability to make a map

None is correct.
None of the statements are false.

Which statement is true for simple random sampling?

Every element has the same chance of being selected

All options are correct.

Samples can be select in order

The results will be biased estimation of the mean of the entire population

Confidence interval indicates the variation of those estimates
Every element has the same chance of being selected.

Burning forested areas to clear land could pollute surrounding rivers via  ________.

increasing evaporation from the newly cleared areas

None is correct.

decreasing transpiration by vegetation

increasing sediment runoff

warming the water, thus reducing the concentration of dissolved oxygen in the water
increasing sediment runoff.

Burning forested areas to clear land could pollute surrounding rivers via ________.

increasing evaporation from the newly cleared areas

None is correct.

decreasing transpiration by vegetation
None of the options are correct.

Forests approximately cover ________% of Canada.

Canadian forests approximately constitute ________% of Earth's forested areas.

70-80; 20

40-50; 10

20-30; 20

60-70; 10

10-20; 10

Forests approximately cover ________% of Canada.

Canadian forests approximately constitute ________% of Earth’s forested areas.

Forests approximately cover 40-50% of Canada.

Canadian forests approximately constitute 10% of Earth's forested areas.

Which of the following criterion is NOT aligned with the Montréal Process?
Institutional framework of conservation should be improved to maintain the sustainability of forest management.

All criteria fall within the agreement.

Logging restrictions should be applied when the activities violated the corresponding legislation in countries of harvest.

The role of forests in carbon cycles need to be maintained in the long term.

Ecosystem productivity, health, and vitality are important facets in forest maintenance.

Societal demands need to be fully considered.

Ecosystem services provided by forests (e.g. biodiversity, soil protection, etc.) need to conserved.
All criteria fall within the agreement.

Which of the following criterion is NOT aligned with the Montréal Process?


Institutional framework of conservation should be improved to maintain the sustainability of forest management.

All criteria fall within the agreement.

Logging restrictions should be applied when the activities violated the corresponding legislation in countries of harvest.

The role of forests in carbon cycles need to be maintained in the long term.

Ecosystem productivity, health, and vitality are important facets in forest maintenance.

Societal demands need to be fully considered.

Ecosystem services provided by forests (e.g. biodiversity, soil protection, etc.) need to conserved.
All criteria fall within the agreement.

Which of the following statement regarding natural forests is incorrect?

Most of the natural forested areas are under legal restrictions for protection.

Natural forests are experiencing habitat fragmentation as well as habitat loss in recent years.

There are more semi-natural forests than undisturbed ones.

Timber harvesting can pose great threats to natural forests in many nations.

Fragmented habitats in natural forests are degrading over time.

Forest loss is one of the major concerns for natural forests worldwide.

All options are correct.
All options are correct.

Which of the following statement regarding natural forests is incorrect?

Most of the natural forested areas are under legal restrictions for protection.

Natural forests are experiencing habitat fragmentation as well as habitat loss in recent years.

There are more semi-natural forests than undisturbed ones.

Timber harvesting can pose great threats to natural forests in many nations.

Fragmented habitats in natural forests are degrading over time.

Forest loss is one of the major concerns for natural forests worldwide.
All the statements are correct.

Which of the following ideas may NOT benefit the remaining natural forests?

All options can benefit the remaining natural forests

Maintaining various harvesting rotations over time in the remaining forests

Long-term landscape planning

Assessment of different ecosystem services that provided by natural forests

Lobbying the authorities against the commercialization of forested areas

Adaptive management of natural disturbances

Appreciating the aesthetic and recreational values of forests
Lobbying the authorities against the commercialization of forested areas may not benefit the remaining natural forests if it leads to a lack of funding for conservation efforts.

Which of the following ideas may NOT benefit the remaining natural forests?

All options can benefit the remaining natural forests

Maintaining various harvesting rotations over time in the remaining forests

Long-term landscape planning

Assessment of different ecosystem services that provided by natural forests
All options can benefit the remaining natural forests.

Considering the objectives of forest management, which of the following is a forest management technique that involves systematically testing different management approaches and learning from approaches that work or don't work?

Multiple-use management (e.g. pulp logs, saw logs, house logs, etc.)

Salvage logging (i.e. removing damaged trees due to disturbances)

None is correct

Timber harvesting with maximum yield

Adaptive management (e.g. site evaluation and treatment)
Adaptive management (e.g. site evaluation and treatment) involves systematically testing different management approaches and learning from approaches that work or don't work.

Which of the following is TRUE of merchantable volume?

The volume includes both commercial and non-commercial value.

It can’t be used to measure the productivity of the landbase.

The volume that can be used to produce wood products, like branches, stumps.

All options are correct.

Merchantable volume means the total annual productivity of the forest.
The volume that can be used to produce wood products, like branches, stumps, and roots is correct.

Salvage logging (i.e. removing damaged trees after the wildfire, storms, or insect outbreaks) does NOT ________.

make sense economically in the short term

create uneven age forest stands

help the plantation in burned forests

reduce the number of habitats for animals

None is correct
None of the options are incorrect.

Salvage logging (i.e. removing damaged trees after the wildfire, storms, or insect outbreaks) does NOT ________.

make sense economically in the short term

create uneven age forest stands

help the plantation in burned forests

reduce the number of habitats for animals

All the statements are incorrect. 

Salvage logging can make sense economically in the short term.

Salvage logging can create uneven age forest stands. 

Salvage logging can help the plantation in burned forests in some cases.

Salvage logging can reduce the number of habitats for animals.
